description:
Please ensure you have read the syllabus before you begin this assessment. If you are unclear on academic integrity you should consult these resources:

  • What is academic integrity at BYU?
  • How to use Gen AI to aid my coursework, learning, and process of becoming?

This class involves you writing some reports. A very useful resource is the Research and Writing Resources. If you need some extra help with academic writing you can visit their site. They have some excellent writing guides and offer appointments.

When it comes to our assignments, it much more useful, efficient, and beneficial for you to do you work and cite work that is not yours. Throughout the class I'll be asking you to generate original ideas. Copying or plagiarizing concepts completely misses the point of that exercise.
name: 🎯 Academic Integrity Quiz

description: I would like a little background information on the class to help me get to know you a little better and for me to tailor some of the class content to your skills and expertise. Please complete this survey prior to class. If you have specific questions or needs to raise with me prior to class please do so ASAP.
name: 🎯 Introduction Survey

description:
Preparation for the Live Class

  • Read the Syllabus
  • Make yourself comfortable with the canvas site
  • Read Bring your breakthrough ideas to life.pdf
  • Ensure you have completed the pre-class materials

The goal of these assignments is to (a) get your into the reading, (b) get you to engage the reading, and (c) allow us to be on the same page for class. I want to see genuine engagement with the work. 

Rubric: 

  • Minimum of ~1-2 paragraphs per question response.
  • Please do not use AI for this--I'm looking for your personal reaction and response to the reading. 
  • Please do not exceed 1 page. If you do, please justify this decision. Justifications might include: "I was on a roll and was inspired by the reading, so I just had to get it out!" 

Remember the goal here--I don't want to 'bog you down' but I do want you to engage prior to class. 

Assignment:

Please answer the following questions. You can enter these as text or submit a file (Word document). Have a better idea? Propose it to me and we'll explore!  

RESPONSE PROMPTS
Submit (via text, document upload, or otherwise) responses to the following questions:

  1. How do you see innovation being relevant to your future work? Please give examples from the article.
  2. What 'gaps' do you see in your current ability to be innovative and how might you make progress toward filling those gaps?
  3. What small, simple things do you commit to do during this semester to increase your creativity, innovativeness, and growth mindset? 

 

↩ 🗂 Session 1: Introduction
name: 📝 Preparation/ Participation - Introduction

description:
Preparation for the Live Class - What is EI? [How I Built This Podcast]

  • Prior to selecting and listening to a HIBT podcast, download the assignment template so you have an idea of what ideas to explore. 
  • Select one of the following How I Built This (HBIT) episodes for this assignment.
    • Kodiak Cakes 
    • Burts Bees
    • 1800 Got Junk
    • Netflix (Diary of a CEO)
  • Then complete and submit this Template: 
    • Be prepared to share your responses from your template in class

Please submit this before the class. Bring a copy (digital or paper) as we will leverage it in the class.

 

↩ 🗂 Session 2: What is EI
name: 📝 Preparation/ Participation - What is EI?--Listen to HIBT Podcast and Submit Form

description:
This assignment is to aid you in better understanding yourself in relation to the core topics of this class: creativity, curiosity, entrepreneurship, innovation, and so forth. 

Assignment:

1) Complete the survey found here: Creativity and Curiosity Survey

2) Once complete, upload your results here (via text or upload)
name: Curiosity-Creativity Self-Evaluation Survey

description:
Preparation for the Live Class

  • Complete the assigned readings listed in this module
    • Session 3 Readings
  • The goal of these assignments is to (a) get your into the reading, (b) get you to engage the reading, and (c) allow us to be on the same page for class. I want to see genuine engagement with the work. 
    • Rubric: 
      • Minimum of ~1-2 paragraphs per question response.
      • Please do not use AI for this--I'm looking for your personal reaction and response to the reading. 
      • Please do not exceed 1 page. If you do, please justify this decision. Justifications might include: "I was on a roll and was inspired by the reading, so I just had to get it out!" 
      • Remember the goal here--I don't want to 'bog you down' but I do want you to engage prior to class. 

Assignment:

Please answer the following questions. You can enter these as text or submit a file (Word document). Have a better idea? Propose it to me and we'll explore!  

RESPONSE PROMPTS
1) From Grant: How is 'rethinking' a skill set and a mindset? Offer examples and application to you.

2) What do you make of Foster's definition(s) of ideas? What are the implications for you and your ability to be an idea creator, initiator, and generator? 

3) What new view of creativity (as a trait? skill? something else) and innovation did you extract from the Kelley & Kelley Creative Confidence sections? 

Please come prepared ready to engage with your classmates in exploring the material.

 

↩ 🗂 Session 03: Cultivating EI (for me): Rethinking and Taking Action
name: 📝 Preparation/ Participation
original_assignment_name: 📝 Preparation/ Participation - What is EI?--Listen to HIBT Podcast and Submit Form

description:
Overview and assignment: We are all capable of identifying customer needs, in part by noting deficiencies in goods and services we use day to day.

1. Bug list component: To prove to yourself that you can identify such needs, please generate a list of at least 20 ‘bugs’ or potential challenges that exist in everyday life. Please upload a completed version of the template here: Overview of the exercise and questions to ask

Want to learn / read more about Bug lists? You can check out these resources: Thinkertoys; Creative Confidence. 

2. Creative Confidence growth plan component: Expanding your creative confidence can be as simple as striving to (a) Observe in new ways, (b) Interview someone to ask questions, and (c) Experience something--first person or via video--to gain a new perspective. Simply list several ways you hope to expand your creative confidence by specifying new ways you can observe, interview, and experience new things as part of your everyday life. Here are some examples and additional details from the last time I did this exercise in Luxembourg City, Luxembourg while teaching a workshop.

What to learn more? You can reference Kelley & Kelley's material for this week (Intro and parts of Ch. 1 from their book) as they detail ways we can cultivate our creative confidence. This is consistent with the notion of "everyday awe"--seeking daily ways to get outside ourself, increase our curiosity, and find novelty in our daily living. Some growing research (Williams & Shepherd, 2025) reveals the link between awe and transformational opportunities. 

You can submit your plan in the text response or in a document.  

Grading Rubric: Grading will primarily be based on:

  • Evidence of sincere effort to identify key problems, NOT the 'monetizability' of the problem.
    • Remember, you're looking for problems, issues, or things that could be fixed, NOT identifying the solution. Problems have many, many solutions, so jumping in too soon misses the exploration. 
  • Degree to which you followed instructions
  • Length: Be succinct and focused.
name: CC Growth Plan and Buglist

description:
Preparation for the Class

  • Read the Case 'Savannah Bananas.'  This case is about innovation and market disruption as viewed through the lens of one of the most entertaining and dynamic sports teams in baseball: the Savannah Bananas. Fans First Entertainment created a modified version of baseball they call "Banana Ball." The goal of this case study is to explore (a) how this innovation emerged, (b) identify potentially repeatable 'common principles' of entrepreneurial innovation, (c) and to consider the roles of diverse stakeholders (the focal organization, customers, existing market competitors/alternatives, baseball players, etc.) in achieving an innovation. 
  • In preparation for the case, please answer prepare responses to the following questions (enter responses via text or submit via a file):
    1. How are the Savannah Bananas innovating the game of baseball and what other organizations can you identify that are innovative / disruptive in similar ways? 
    2. When creating something new 'failure' is inevitable. What was SB's approach to failure and why was that important? Please consider examples of how their approach to failing/failure is similar to or different from organizations you've been part of. 
    3. How did SB prioritize needs of different stakeholder: fans first, investors, other groups? What do you make of their "Five E" leadership principles
    4. What role does an organization's culture play in cultivating innovation? What factors do you see as being important or simply idiosyncratic to SB? 
    5. As time permits--reflect on what SB might do next in expanding its innovative approach to baseball without losing the very value that led to its emergence. 

Please come prepared ready to engage with your classmates in exploring the material.

 

↩ 🗂 Session 04 (Contexts, stakeholders, and processes of EI)
name: 📝 Preparation/ Participation

description:
Preparation for the Live Class

  • Complete the assigned readings listed in this module
  • Answer the following questions (enter answers via text or submit via a file). Please come ready to use the frameworks we read about in our class discussion. 
    • What is your understanding of Jobs-to-be-done and when might you see the framework (in full or partial form) as being useful for problem-solving?
    • What connections do you see between:
      • Jobs to be done and Value Proposition 
      • Value proposition and principles of iteration
    • Please list 1-2 examples that resonated with you from any of the reading, explaining the principle from the framework that links to the example. 

Please come prepared ready to engage with your classmates in exploring the material.

 

↩ 🗂 Session 05: Producing ideas by fulfilling jobs to be done
name: 📝 Preparation/ Participation

description:
Overview and assignment: 

  • Prior to beginning the assignment, ensure you have read and engaged the reading materials
  • Review this short article and watch the short video on Google Glass listed in this session's module. 
  • After watching the video, please fill out and submit the following template. If you have questions, first please review the material! The main objective here is to get your practicing with the notion of thinking about a 'job' the product does (i.e., the need it fulfills) RATHER THAN just the product itself.  

You can submit your plan in the text response or in a document.  

Grading: Grading will primarily be based on:

  • Evidence of sincere effort to identify key problems
  • The level of detail and effort demonstrated
  • Degree to which you followed instructions
name: Case Prep--Applying JBTD and Value Prop Assignment

description:
Preparation for the Live Class

  • Complete the assigned readings listed in this module (excerpts from Creative Construction and Safi Bahcall's talk on Loonshots) 
  • Answer the following questions (enter answers via text or submit via a file)

    1) Who actually “owns” innovation at different stages—and how does that ownership shift?

      • Drawing on Creative Construction (Chs. 5–6 and 8–10) and Bahcall’s distinction between loonshots and franchises, identify key stakeholders involved at idea creation, validation, scaling, and institutionalization. In considering the question above, you might explore which stakeholders add value at each stage, and which tend to unintentionally suppress innovation? How should a leader change their behavior as ownership shifts (e.g., from explorer to translator to protector)?

       2) When does resistance to innovation signal incompetence—and when does it signal responsibility? 

      • Both Creative Construction and Bahcall suggest that resistance often comes from rational actors protecting legitimate interests (capital, reputation, customers, employees). To answer the above question you might do the following: 

        • Choose one stakeholder group (e.g., middle managers, regulators, incumbent business units, investors). The ask: Under what conditions is their resistance healthy rather than regressive? How should innovators reframe their thinking to collaborate with—not bypass or villainize—this group?

        3) What mental model must leaders unlearn to keep loonshots alive without destroying the organization? 

    • What deeply held beliefs (e.g., efficiency first, ROI certainty, org charts, 'talent solves everything' etc.) become problematic and even dangerous in attempting to scale innovation? Try and compare and contrast some of the ideas here with other material (Grant readings, etc.). How do we get better at asking / answering 'what is needed here' rather than falling back on assumptions?

Please come prepared ready to engage with your classmates in exploring the material.

 

↩ 🗂 Session 06
name: 📝 Preparation/ Participation

description:
Preparation for the Live Class

  • Please prepare by:
    • Watching the Design Sprint overview video files from Jake Knapp and team (~50 mins--you can watch at double or triple speed). While there have been significant updates to the process since it was released, I find that these original videos are helpful in explaining that gap it was filling. You will notice similarities to Agile and other approaches, albeit with key differences that we will uncover and explore. 
    • Watching the interview files (just a quick overview--can dig into this more) from Angel Studios. 
  • Please review the PDF file I have shared that gives some overview information. 

 

Answer the following questions (enter answers via text or submit via a file)

    • What are the features of situations / problems that are best addressed with the Sprint process as opposed to other decision-making frameworks? 
    • Please document a list of ideas (start with 3-5, but don't stop if you feel inspired) you believe represent the problems Angel Studios is facing? Don't focus on solutions, just try and build out / document problems at this point. 

Please come prepared ready to engage with your classmates in exploring the material.

 

↩ 🗂 Session 07
name: 📝 Preparation/ Participation-Section 002

description:
Participation and professionalism—Posted for mid-term (0-30 points) and term end (0-30 pts)
A baseline expectation of this course is that you attend and participate. The ability to ‘think on your feet,’ constructively engage in a debate on class topics, and persuasively and succinctly articulate your point of view is critical for entrepreneurs, product managers and honestly for just about any profession and as a general life skill.

What I will be looking for in class discussions is the extent to which what you add to our discussions contributes to the learning of others in the class. If you are by nature a quiet person, please come talk to me and I’ll help bring you into our class discussions.Good comments possess one or more of the following attributes:

•       Relate to the topic at hand and/or integrate the topic with other course content.

•       Are succinct and avoid hogging attention, time, or one’s point of view (i.e., you acknowledge others in the conversation).

•       Offer a different (sometimes contrarian), unique, and relevant perspective on the issue.

•       Contribute to moving the discussion forward.

•       Build on but don’t repeat other comments (too often we fail to listen to the comments of others).

•       Transcend the “I feel” syndrome; that is, they include some evidence or analysis of inherent tradeoffs.  In other words, they demonstrate some reflective thinking. 

•       Offer solutions and /or possibilities rather than merely a critique. As the saying goes (in various forms)—“any idiot can tear down a barn, but it takes a carpenter to build one.” Pointing out flaws, errors, and so forth is easy and not that interesting. Offering alternatives that can be critiqued takes effort, courage, and helps move our conversations forward.  

Assessing participation (5 points each for 11 class sessions)

I will grade each student’s contribution immediately following class and will post your score at mid and end of term (i.e., two scores ranging from 0-30 points). If you would like to know the status of your participation score, please come speak with me.

Absences, tardiness, and leaving early. Attendance is required for this course, so the absolute floor for participation and professionalism is showing up! I allow 1 excused absence (for any reasons, so use it wisely) throughout the semester as a general policy. If you have unique circumstances—please communicate those as early as possible. Missing classes beyond the 1 excused absence will result in a 3% decrease in your final grade (e.g., 95% --> 92% and so on). University approved absences and emergencies will be handled on a case-by-case basis. Again, just remember—use your one freebie wisely.

Showing up late and/or leaving early is disruptive to your fellow students, teams, and me. While I generally handle these issues on a case-by-case basis, if you are later than 5 minutes to class (without letting me know) and/or leave early (without alerting me to the situation) then you will be marked absent for the day.

Daily scoring for professionalism and participation. During each class I will make notes about participation. I will score participation in the following way:

  • You begin each session with a baseline score of 3 out of 5—that is for attending. Your score can then go up or down depending on the professionalism and quality of your participation.

  • Factors decreasing your score. Unprofessional conduct (i.e., demeaning others, hogging the conversation, etc.) and/or unproductive comments (i.e., simply re-stating someone’s remarks to ‘check the box’) can result in your score going down from 3 to 2 or 1. Similarly, inappropriate use of technology (i.e., violating BYU-Marriott’s phone policy; distracting yourself/others with off-topic computer usage, etc.) will decrease your score.

  • Factors increasing your score. Professional and ‘good comments’ (see above) will result in an increase from the baseline 3 up to 4 or 5.

Please do not overly worry about getting a ‘perfect’ participation score. The goal is to cultivate a community of learning where we engage the content together, integrate our understanding with one another, HAVE FUN, and take our learning to the next level. Let’s all commit to creating an environment for this to happen.
name: Class Participation (Mid-term)

description:
Preparation for the Live Class

Building off our overview class (previous session), please come prepared with the following so you can jump in as a team during class: 

Individually consider the following about Angel Studios and submit some brief notes on the following. Remember, this is problem definition, not solutioning. Your notes will be used in class for the Map and Sketch stages of the Design Sprint:

  • Their core business model and value proposition (you can draw on prior frameworks for this, including the business model canvas) to help you think about imagine different problem fields across stakeholders. 

  • Key stakeholders and what each is optimizing for? What are the 'jobs they need done' etc. 

  • 2–4 major problems or tensions the company is facing--what do you feel are the most important issues they should tackle in the next 6 months to a year? 

  • Turn problems into questions: Ask 'how my we' questions (as many as you'd like!) to try and identify the most pressing questions Angel should tackle next. 

Please submit this prior to class and bring a copy to refer to during team work. Please come prepared ready to engage with your classmates in exploring the material. 

↩ 🗂 Session 08
name: 📝 Preparation/ Participation-Section 02
